title: Cycler Finder Assumed-Errata Ledger
description: Schema v1.0 for data/errata.yaml (task #228): the project's source-defect ledger as a first-class, publishable artifact. Each entry records a printed value in a published source that our reproduction or an internal-consistency argument indicates is defective, together with the full falsifiable reasoning and the in-repo evidence trail (mining note, pinning test, adjudication commit). These are ASSUMED errata: the ledger exists so authors can verify or refute each item, and every entry starts status: open. Tone rule: reasoning must be evidence-first and falsifiable ('the printed value appears inconsistent with X; we derive Y by Z'), never an accusation; unresolved entries are explicitly questions for the authors. Citation rule: doi_or_url is publication-level only (DOI / arXiv / publisher or author page) — never a local file path or private repository.

id.description: Stable kebab-case slug for this erratum (unique across the ledger; uniqueness enforced by tests/data/test_errata.py).

paper.description: Full citation of the published source (authors, title, venue, year).

doi_or_url.description: Publication-level link only: DOI resolver, arXiv abstract, or publisher/author page. Never a local file path or a private repository.

location.description: Where in the source the printed value sits: table / equation / page (use the pagination the entry's citation declares, e.g. arXiv vs VoR).

printed_value.description: The value exactly as printed in the source.

derived_value.description: The value we derive (with one-line method), or the question we cannot resolve for unresolved-discrepancy entries.

reasoning.description: The full falsifiable argument: reproduction method and/or internal-consistency proof. Evidence-first phrasing; framed so the authors can check (and refute) it.

evidence_refs.description: In-repo evidence trail: mining-note path + section, pinning-test node id, and/or commit SHA where the defect was adjudicated. Paths must live in the public repo (docs/notes/, tests/, src/).

confidence.description: confirmed-in-print = the page was re-read twice AND an independent reproduction disagrees with the printed value. provable-internal-inconsistency = the source is mathematically impossible or self-contradictory as printed (e.g. a max exceeding a sum, two printed statements of the same quantity that disagree). unresolved-discrepancy = our reproduction disagrees but we hold no independent proof — phrased as a question for the authors.

affected_catalogue_rows.description: Ids of data/catalogue.yaml rows that inherited or annotate this printed value (may be empty). Referential integrity enforced by tests/data/test_errata.py.

vor_status.description: final-form = the cited document is the version of record (dissertation, technical report, published journal article). preprint-may-be-fixed-in-vor = we read a preprint/author draft; the defect may already be fixed in the (unchecked) version of record.

vor_note.description: Optional note on the version-of-record situation, e.g. 'JGCD VoR DOI 10.2514/1.G008387 unchecked (paywalled)'.

status.description: Lifecycle of the assumed erratum. All entries start open; author contact or a VoR check moves them.

minor.description: Optional flag: true for minor/typographical items (e.g. misspelled author names) that carry no numerical consequence.
